author: Marcus Chen
title: Zero-Click Search Is at 65% — Here's How to Win Anyway
description: Most search queries now end without a click. The brands still winning organic traffic have restructured their entire content strategy around this reality.

sections.p: SparkToro's latest study put zero-click search at 65% of all Google queries. That number will only grow as AI Overviews, featured snippets, and knowledge panels continue expanding. The brands still growing organic traffic have accepted this reality and restructured accordingly — rather than optimizing for clicks they'll never get, they're optimizing for visibility that converts later.

Zero-click doesn't mean zero-value. A brand that appears in Google's AI Overview for 'best CRM for startups' gets a brand impression even if the user never clicks. Enough of those impressions, across enough queries, builds the kind of ambient brand recognition that shortens sales cycles.

The metric that matters isn't traffic from that query — it's share of voice across the query cluster. Track the percentage of relevant queries where your brand, or a reference to your brand, appears in the first visible result.

list: Direct-answer format: lead with the answer, support it below. Google rewards content that is immediately useful, not teased.
Structured data on everything: FAQ, HowTo, and Article schemas make it easier for Google to extract and display your content.
Comparison tables: AI Overviews regularly surface comparison content. A well-structured table beats three paragraphs of prose.
Definition paragraphs: 40–50 words that clearly define a concept are featured snippet gold.
Numbered lists for process queries: 'how to' queries almost always show a list. Format accordingly.

sections.h2: Click traffic that still comes through

sections.p: Zero-click search is highest on informational queries. Commercial-intent queries — 'pricing,' 'buy,' 'best X for Y,' 'alternatives to Z' — still generate clicks at much higher rates. A smart content strategy doubles down on commercial-intent content and accepts that informational content is playing a brand visibility game, not a traffic game.

As Perplexity, ChatGPT, and Gemini answer more queries directly, Answer Engine Optimization becomes as important as SEO. The structural requirements overlap heavily — direct answers, authoritative sourcing, clear entity relationships — but AEO adds a layer: you need to be the kind of source that LLMs are trained to trust.

That means citations from respected publications, consistent positioning language across the web, and structured content that makes it easy for a model to extract and reproduce your claims accurately.

sections.quote: Zero-click isn't the death of SEO. It's SEO evolving from traffic optimization to visibility optimization. The brands that get that reframe win.
